name: Ledger — Docs
title: Docs site
summary: A multi-page documentation site for an open-source library — grouped sidebar nav, getting-started and guides, an API reference, build-time syntax-highlighted code, a working ⌘K search, and a version dropdown.
setup_questions: What's the library/product and a one-line description of what it does?
What are the top 4–5 doc sections (e.g. Getting started, Guides, API reference, Changelog)?
Give me one real code example to feature on the landing page.
(Optional) What's the brand accent color, and the GitHub / npm links?
photo_terms: abstract code editor screen dark theme
sections: Landing page — hero with install command, feature grid, code panels, stats, and next-steps cards
Docs shell — grouped sidebar nav, article column, and a scroll-spy 'On this page' TOC
Getting started — install, create a store, first reactive query, React binding
Guides — reactive queries, persistence, and schema migrations with prose, tables, and callouts
API reference — function signatures, option tables, and per-method anchors
Syntax-highlighted code blocks with filename bar, language label, and copy button
Search modal (⌘K / slash) over a static index with full keyboard navigation
Version dropdown with an older-version banner
based_on: A realistic, evergreen open-source documentation site in the mold of Astro Starlight / VitePress / Docusaurus — landing page, sidebar nav, getting-started, guides, API reference, and changelog for a fictional TypeScript library.
